name: Bhai Joginder & Mohinder Singh (Hazoor Sahib)
body: Bhai Joginder Singh and Bhai Mohinder Singh were legendary figures in the field of music, especially in Kirtan. Music has been in their family for generations as they belong to the Rababi tradition of music dating back a few hundred years. Both brothers were born in Nankana Sahib, which is now in Pakistan. The older of the two brothers, Bhai Joginder Singh Ji was born in 1940, followed by the birth of Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji in 1944.

Their father, Bhai Tarlochan Singh Mastana served as a Hazoori Raagi in Gurdwara Janam Asthan Nankana Sahib prior to the partition of Punjab in 1947. He was also a renowned ‘tabla vadak’ and had accompanied the jatha of Bhai Jaswant Singh and Bhai Pal Singh. In one of his recordings, Bhai Pal Singh Ji mentioned that Bhai Tarlochan Singh Mastana would often sit with Akhtari Bai ‘Faizabadi’ and sing with her. He also said that the singing of Bhai Prithipal Singh and Bhai Mohanpal Singh was inspired by Bhai Tarlochan Singh Ji. It is said that his voice could go well beyond the upper notes of the harmonium.

Going back another generation, Bhai Tarlochan Singh’s father, Bhai Kima and uncle, Bhai Molu were accomplished singers as well. They were popularly known as the jatha of ‘Molu Kima’ and it is said that there is a street named after them in Pakistan. Both Bhai Molu and Bhai Kima became Amritdhari Sikhs and changed their names to Bhai Sant Singh and Bhai Basant Singh respectively. After the partition of Punjab, Bhai Tarlochan Singh Ji and his family settled in Patiala along with three other Rababi families from Nankana Sahib; Bhai Gurmukh Singh & Sarmukh Singh Fakkar, Bhai Jaswant Singh & Pal Singh and Bhai Mehar Singh Taan. During this time, Bhai Tarlochan Singh Ji got separated from his younger brother, Baba Manga Ji who got left behind in newly formed Pakistan. Sadly, they would never meet again.

Coming back to Bhai Joginder Singh Ji and Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji, they received their ‘taleem’ from home, mainly from their father. They also went on to learn from Ustad Bade Ghulam Ali Khan of Patiala Gharana. In 1976, Bhai Joginder Singh and Bhai Mohinder Singh accompanied ‘Jathedar’ Baba Santa Singh Ji of 96 Crori Panth Akali Budha Dal to Sachkhand Sri Hazoor Sahib, Nanded. During this visit, the Sangat of Nanded were so mesmerized by their Kirtan that they were requested to stay on. Since then, their family has been serving Sachkhand Sri Hazoor Sahib.

Bhai Joginder Singh Ji was the jathedar, while Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji used to provide accompaniment on dilruba and vocals. They were initially accompanied on tabla by Bhai Mahinderpal Singh ‘Ladoo’, and subsequently by their own sons. Bhai Joginder Singh Ji passed away in 1985 at the very young age of 45. After his passing, Bhai Mohinder Singh continued to perform Kirtan with his sons until he too passed away in 1996. The information above was obtained from Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji’s son, Bhai Gurpartap Singh and some details were also taken from the book ‘Gurmat Sangeet De Anmol Ratan’ by  late Bhai Nirmal Singh Khalsa.

One other detail that I would like to share was narrated to me by Bhai Kishanpal Singh Ji and Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji. Once, when Ustad Salamat Ali Khan of Sham Chaurasi Gharana was visiting their home in Patiala, he listened to a cassette that Bhai Kishanpal Singh Ji had put on.  In this cassette, Bhai Joginder Singh and Bhai Mohinder Singh were singing ‘Deen Duniya Dar Kamande’ in Raag Vachaspati. He listened to the entire recording and at the end, he said that although he has sung the same Raag before, he could not fill the same ‘Rang’ into the Raag as they had done. It is wonderful to see the respect one artist has for another and the humbleness of an artist of such stature. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Joginder Singh and Bhai Mohinder Singh to share, please contact us.
